questions.question: Active: 'She writes a letter.' Convert to Passive:
options: A letter is written by her
A letter was written by her
A letter has been written by her
A letter is being written by her
questions.correctAnswer: A letter is written by her
questions.explanation: Simple present active → simple present passive: is + past participle + by + subject.

questions.question: Passive: 'The book was read by John.' Convert to Active:
options: John reads the book
John will read the book
John read the book
John has read the book
questions.explanation: Was read (simple past passive) → read (simple past active).

questions.question: Active: 'They will complete the project.' Convert to Passive:
options: The project will be completed by them
The project will completed by them
The project is completed by them
The project has been completed by them
questions.correctAnswer: The project will be completed by them
questions.explanation: Future active → will + be + past participle + by.

questions.question: Active: 'She is cooking dinner.' Convert to Passive:
options: Dinner is being cooked by her
Dinner was being cooked by her
Dinner is cooked by her
Dinner has been cooked by her
questions.correctAnswer: Dinner is being cooked by her
questions.explanation: Present continuous active → is being + past participle + by.

questions.question: Passive: 'A new bridge is being built.' Who built it is:
options: Unknown from the sentence
Engineers
The government
Workers
questions.explanation: When the agent (by whom) is absent in passive, the actor is unknown.

questions.question: Active: 'Did he solve the problem?' In Passive:
options: Was the problem solved by him?
Has the problem been solved by him?
Is the problem solved by him?
Will the problem be solved by him?
questions.correctAnswer: Was the problem solved by him?
questions.explanation: Past interrogative passive: Was + object + past participle + by subject?

questions.question: Active: 'They have elected a new president.' In Passive:
options: A new president was elected by them
A new president is elected by them
A new president has been elected by them
A new president had been elected by them
questions.correctAnswer: A new president has been elected by them
questions.explanation: Present perfect active → has been + past participle.
